title: Climate risks to Syria’s urban water and sanitation systems
content: This briefing paper presents key findings from a semi-quantitative climate risk assessment of Syria's urban drinking water and sanitation, intended to support the country's reconstruction efforts.
snippet: This briefing paper presents key findings from a semi-quantitative climate risk assessment of Syria's urban drinking water and sanitation, intended to support the country's reconstruction efforts.

title: How to manage crises differently in ASALs without talking about a nexus: what we can learn from the water sector
content: This policy brief examines the provision of water in the arid and semi-arid lands of eastern Africa, offering a different way of addressing a long-standing problem.
snippet: This policy brief examines the provision of water in the arid and semi-arid lands of eastern Africa, offering a different way of addressing a long-standing problem.

title: Addressing spiralling climate and environmental risks in Yemen's Taizz and Hadhramaut governates
content: This report shares evidence on climate-related hazards that could worsen conflict and security risks in Yemen, in order to inform stabilisation, peacebuilding, and security interventions
snippet: This report shares evidence on climate-related hazards that could worsen conflict and security risks in Yemen, in order to inform stabilisation, peacebuilding, and security interventions

title: Strategic roadmap for localising early warning systems

title: Ahmedabad ground sinking signals climate infrastructure risks
content: New satellite-based research has identified signs of Ahmedabad land subsidence across parts of the city’s western and southern urban corridors, raising concerns over groundwater management, urban planning and the long-term resilience.

title: Climate action as peace enabler: lessons from the Sahel on delivering peace and social dividends in fragile settings
content: This case study examines the Justice and Stability in the Sahel (JASS) programme in Mali and Niger to explore whether climate action in the Sahel can generate broader peace and social benefits alongside enhanced resilience.
snippet: This case study examines the Justice and Stability in the Sahel (JASS) programme in Mali and Niger to explore whether climate action in the Sahel can generate broader peace and social benefits

title: Record-breaking heat spreads through Europe
content: An extraordinary heatwave in Europe has shattered numerous temperature records and had major impacts on human health, ecosystems, agriculture, infrastructure and labour productivity.
snippet: An extraordinary heatwave in Europe has shattered numerous temperature records and had major impacts on human health, ecosystems, agriculture, infrastructure and labour productivity.

title: Assessment tool for hospital preparedness for heatwaves

title: Across Europe, heat adaptation plans are being put to a brutal test
content: Cities across the world are still preparing for the heat that we're experiencing today.

title: As communities face more frequent hazard warnings, we need better systems to avoid ‘emergency fatigue’

title: Could this be Australia’s warmest winter ever?
content: Many parts of Australia have already broken early winter maximum and minimum temperature records.

title: Yale’s new extreme weather dashboard could help older adults understand climate health risks

title: Disasters destroy more than homes – they scatter communities too
content: Evidence from Uganda suggests that disasters can reduce income and life satisfaction for years, especially when households are displaced without their social networks.
snippet: Evidence from Uganda suggests that disasters can reduce income and life satisfaction for years, especially when households are displaced without their social networks.

title: A severe El Niño could threaten something essential to half of humanity – rice
content: Forecasters expect the El Niño now underway in the tropical Pacific to strengthen into a strong or very strong climate driver later this year.
snippet: Forecasters expect the El Niño now underway in the tropical Pacific to strengthen into a strong or very strong climate driver later this year.

title: Preventing and managing climate-related insecurity: lessons from the lake Chad basin regional strategy
content: The lives and livelihoods of local communities in the borderlands of the Lake Chad Basin are disrupted by both climate change and conflict , which are mutually reinforcing.
snippet: The lives and livelihoods of local communities in the borderlands of the Lake Chad Basin are disrupted by both climate change and conflict , which are mutually reinforcing.

title: When metrics miss the point: why climate adaptation metrics must reflect plural worldviews
content: Climate change adaptation ‘success’ is being defined and measured through metrics that often fail the communities they are meant to support.

title: How cultural spaces offer a cool escape from the heat

title: Zambia crisis and disaster risk financing diagnostic

title: Unbearable heat for hundreds of millions
content: The Global South, in particular, is suffering from the consequences of global heating. A new study quantifies heat stress in India by projecting it during the critical seasons.
snippet: The Global South, in particular, is suffering from the consequences of global heating. A new study quantifies heat stress in India by projecting it during the critical seasons.

title: ‘This was something new’: what people in Greenland told us about learning to live with wildfires
content: Scientists have warned for years that climate change would increase wildfires across the Arctic. In places such as Siberia it’s already happening. In that context, the 2015-2020 fires may represent Greenland following the same path.

title: Risk analysis of infrastructure vulnerability and sustainable resource exploitation in the Arctic

title: A hotter, drier 2026 monsoon can compound disaster risks in the Hindu Kush Himalaya
content: <p>The HKH Monsoon Outlook 2026 forecasts that several countries in the Hindu Kush Himalaya (HKH) region, including Bhutan, India, Nepal, and Pakistan, are likely to witness drier-than-average monsoon conditions coupled with temperatures averaging 0.5&nbsp;°C to 2&nbsp;°C above the seasonal normal.&nbsp;</p><p>A below-normal monsoon with above-normal seasonal temperature may compound disaster vulnerabilities across the HKH region, warns scientists from the International Centre for Integrated Mountain Development (ICIMOD), a co-publisher of this year’s Outlook.&

title: Venezuela’s deadly earthquakes happened on a fault similar to the San Andreas, and the risks aren’t over yet – a geophysicist explains

title: Summer’s new normal is a hazard that’s testing Europe’s climate resilience

title: Extreme heat is muddling animals’ brains—and even triggering aggression
content: When it’s hot outside, birds struggle to learn, dogs bite more often, goat-like chamois pick fights.
